title: passiflora hirsuta var. parviflora
intro: Passiflora hirsuta var. parviflora, also known as the dwarf passionflower or passionflower vine, is a species of flowering plant in the passionflower family native to tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and sedative effects. The health benefits of Passiflora hirsuta var. parviflora include relief from anxiety and insomnia, as well as pain relief and improved sleep quality. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used in combination with other herbs for a synergistic effect.
